content:
type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Workable, with precise line targets to avoid collision: (1) __init__ signature: I'll insert 'debug: bool = False, debug_logger: logging.Logger | None = None' right after allow_reuse param; you then append idle_timeout_s as the new last param after mine. (2) __init__ body: I'll add self._logger = debug_logger or logging.getLogger(...) right before 'self.adapter_identifiers = {' (i.e. after the blank line following allow_reuse) -- NOT touching your self._last_chunk_ts line placed right after 'self.allow_reuse = allow_reuse'. (3) In receive(): I add a 'reset' debug log inside the allow_reuse block (lines ~87-93, e.g. after 'self.stream_start = False') -- you said you won't touch that block, good. (4) I add a 'start detection' log right after the start_identifier is matched (~line 136-140, before the elif at 142) -- before your 151-169 zone, no overlap. (5) I add a 'rolling end check' log on its own new line immediately after 'self.stream_end = True' (~line 163) and BEFORE 'last_token = self.flush()' -- a single narrow insert, not touching your ~168 area. (6) For emit-chunk logging: I'll add my log as the very FIRST line inside the 'if token:' block (right after line 168), then your self._last_chunk_ts update line can follow immediately after mine, then the existing return statement -- sequential inserts, no shared-line edits. (7) flush() method: I add logging inside flush() body (start/before returns) -- doesn't conflict with your tick() method since you're inserting tick() as a whole new method between receive() and flush(), not editing flush() itself. ACCEPT if workable.
